text:
Market today: Mixed signals for your book. Memory/chip names are getting a lift from Micron's blowout quarter - that's a tailwind for your chip-heavy portfolio. But a potential Warsh rate hike green light is a real headwind; higher rates hit growth/tech multiples hard, and that's exactly what you're concentrated in. Iran war spending is adding macro noise.

Your holdings: PLTR is your loudest alarm - down 12.77% and on a SELL signal; on margin that hole gets deeper fast, and your stop at $85 is still far below. AVGO is your largest position, also down 12.45% with a TRIM signal - size plus loss plus margin is a dangerous combo, trim first. SPAL is down 14.48% already; it's a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily, not a hold-and-hope name. SPCX is brand-new and very volatile - keep size tiny.

Watch: 1) Any Fed/Warsh commentary on rate hikes - that's the single biggest risk to your entire book. 2) Whether chip momentum from Micron lifts AVGO and SMH enough to give you a better exit price on AVGO.

Bottom line: Your margin account can't afford to let PLTR and AVGO bleed together - one of them needs action today.
